module: platform-feature (operates on dropped components in a Generic Step column)
qcSource: Author Test Cases - Copy and Paste.pdf
architectureNotes: Copy/Paste is NOT its own widget — it operates on existing dropped components. The reference siblings (extended-response, text) supply the drop + step helpers reused here; the copy source is a Fill in the Blank widget in a Generic Step column.

Per-component heading toolbar (rendered by findTag): .duplicate-widget-icon.icon-Page-duplication-01 (componentDuplicate, always present) and .copy-widget-icon.icon-Copy-Icon (componentCopy). The ORIGINAL drop template (ngcontroller.js ~2341) renders only duplicate + delete, so the copy icon is build-dependent — helpers degrade gracefully when absent.

Clipboard is localStorage-backed: componentCopy clears localStorage then writes copiedComponent / targetDataType / copiedTemplates / isDroppable. Copy → paste round trips are driveable (NOT OS clipboard).

Paste target is the empty Generic Step slot (.generic-step-empty-droppable-text): hover sets questions.showPasteHere → renders .icon-Paste-Icon + 'Paste Here'; ng-click="pastecomp($event)" → componentPaste + onPasteDropComplete.
Invalid paste targets (TOC panel / plain Instructions canvas) have no pastecomp binding, so a copied component cannot be pasted there (TC_16).

Success/status toast renders into '.bb-alert' (span.textContent + span.alert-icon) with class 'alert-success'. componentCopy => 'The component has been copied successfully.'; componentDuplicate => 'Component duplicated successfully.'
Save/Publish, Reader (CLP) verification, cross-program parity (ELA/WL/MATH), undo, persistence-after-republish, OS-clipboard Ctrl+C/V, and a11y are deferred (flows outside the authoring DOM).
